roster_detail.name: Amon-Ra St. Brown
roster_detail.scouting: Bullish: elite, high-floor target earner (top YPRR/TPRR); the mild concern is Ben Johnson's departure changing the scheme, but role and efficiency are locked.
roster_detail.quote: Did Ben Johnson's slot WR get 1,000 yards or finish top-15 among all WRs in receptions? 2012: Brian Hartline -✅ 2013: Br

Amon-Ra St. Brown: 3.09 with, 2.00 w…

I have him ranked as the WR4 ahead of 2026.

roster_detail.name: Tyler Warren
roster_detail.scouting: Bullish sleeper TE: athletic with a strong YAC profile in a loaded second-year TE class; an ascending IND target whose ceiling rides the offense/QB.

tweets.t:
Tyler Warren’s rookie season was a MASSIVE success.

Set team records with 76 catches and 817 yards.

5 touchdowns ranks 2nd for a Colts rookie. 

Led the offense with 112 targets. 

When targeting tight ends we always want #1 or #2 options
relentlessly drafting Tyler Warren right now

Also rewatched every Tyler Warren target.

He's probably 85% as effective as Harold Fannin Jr. with the ball in his hands, 80% as effective as a route-runner, and 150% as effective as a catcher of the football.

Warren is so good tracking/c
Tyler Warren iso win vs Pat Surtain on 3rd and 2 https://t.co/HgCVf4rfNs
Tyler Warren changing direction like this at 256 lbs is quite impressive https://t.co/Us6vtKGjPp

roster_detail.name: Justin Herbert
roster_detail.scouting: Mixed / trap: a big arm with sneaky scramble rushing, but the board flags him a trap (prefers Michael Wilson at cost) - a fine but unexciting QB.

Very logical footwork change for Justin Herbert - setting up with his left foot forward in shotgun instead of his right

For a righty QB that puts his lead foot closer to throwing position at the snap so he can get the ball out quicker for
